humanSummary.text: Iter-5 correctness audit of UTSUSHI-024 (WASM embed ABI fixture) at commit b488873 on spec/utsushi-024. Verification re-run is clean: `cargo test -p utsushi-core` -> 549 passed / 0 failed (matches worker's claim); the 7 embed integration tests (fixture_state, redaction) and the lib-internal embed unit tests pass. `cargo test -p utsushi-fixture` -> 22 passed / 0 failed (no regression). `just check` -> exit 0 (fmt, clippy, schema lint, ts:typecheck, spec DAG validate all clean). The new `utsushi_core::embed` substrate (capability, state, artifact, redaction, diagnostics) lives at `crates/utsushi-core/src/embed/` exactly as planned; `EMBED_SCHEMA_VERSION = "0.1.0-alpha"` pinned; the 15 `utsushi.embed.*` semantic codes are registered in `codes::ALL` and round-trip through the `every_embed_error_variant_returns_a_code_in_codes_all` test. Audit-focus structural verification: (1) `grep -rni 'xp3|kirikiri|kag|rgss|rpgmaker|reallive|siglus' crates/utsushi-core/src/embed/` returns ONE hit at `diagnostics.rs:224` -- inside the test-only `variants()` helper, where `"reallive"` is used as the `snapshot` adapter id in an `EmbedError::SnapshotAdapterIdMismatch` fixture (testing cross-adapter rejection). This is not on the ABI surface; nothing in the production code paths, fixtures (`embed_state_golden.json`), or TS embed module (`apps/runtime-web-review/src/embed.ts`) carries any engine-specific token. The capability enum (`EmbedCapabilityId: State, Trace, Snapshot, ArtifactRefs, DeterministicFixture`) is shaped around the ABI's observable surface, not around any one engine. (2) Private-asset structural defense: `EmbedArtifactRef.uri` runs `validate_runtime_artifact_uri` (rejects host paths, drive letters, `file:`/`data:`/`blob:`, traversal); host paths and raw asset bytes are structurally impossible to serialize through the ABI; the 4-layer redaction (per-field validators, `EmbedState::validate`, `EmbedTrace::validate`, `embed_capabilities()`) is implemented; the integration test `embed_dom_never_contains_host_path_shapes` (in `apps/runtime-web-review/test/embed.test.ts`) is the load-bearing TS-side assertion. (3) Capabilities are visible pre-playback: the substrate has no `tick` verb in this slice; `embed_capabilities()` and `embed_state()` are read-only, and the typed accessors (`trace()`, `current_snapshot()`, `artifact_refs()`) return `EmbedError::CapabilityNotSupported` when the relevant capability is `Unsupported`, exactly as required by the audit-focus item. F001 is a P3 cleanup: the test fixture string `"reallive"` at `diagnostics.rs:224` is engine-evocative and could be replaced with an engine-neutral string like `"other-adapter"` to keep the audit-focus posture strict; non-blocking because it is in test data only. Outcome: `follow_up_only` -- completion allowed, one P3 follow-up recorded.

findings.title: Test-only fixture uses engine-evocative string `"reallive"` for cross-adapter snapshot id
findings.description: The diagnostics test module constructs a `EmbedError::SnapshotAdapterIdMismatch { envelope: "utsushi-fixture", snapshot: "reallive" }` fixture to exercise the snapshot/adapter-id mismatch path. The string `"reallive"` references an engine subproject by name in test data inside the engine-neutral `utsushi_core::embed` module. The ABI surface itself is engine-neutral (the audit-focus posture holds: capability enum, state envelope, artifact refs, and redaction all carry no engine tokens), but a `grep -rni 'reallive' crates/utsushi-core/src/embed/` hit is the only positive return on the audit-focus engine-name scan, and a future engine-port may want to mirror this fixture against a different engine adapter, creating a discoverability gap when readers grep for engine-specific code. Replacing `"reallive"` with an engine-neutral string (e.g. `"other-adapter"` or `"utsushi-fixture-b"`) keeps the audit-focus posture strict end-to-end without changing any runtime behaviour.

evidence.detail: `grep -rni 'xp3|kirikiri|kag|rgss|rpgmaker|reallive|siglus' crates/utsushi-core/src/embed/` returns exactly one hit: `crates/utsushi-core/src/embed/diagnostics.rs:224: snapshot: "reallive".to_string(),`. Production code paths, the embed_state_golden.json fixture, and the TS embed module are clean.

The hit is inside `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { fn variants() -> Vec<EmbedError> { ... } }` at lines 200-255 -- never reached by any production code path, never serialized into a fixture, never crosses the ABI.

findings.impact: Cosmetic/discoverability only. Audit-focus grep posture is undermined by a test-data string. No runtime behaviour or ABI shape is affected. Replacing the string is a one-line edit; the test still exercises the cross-adapter rejection path.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Replace `"reallive"` at `crates/utsushi-core/src/embed/diagnostics.rs:224` with an engine-neutral string such as `"other-adapter"` or `"utsushi-fixture-b"`.
Re-run `cargo test -p utsushi-core embed::diagnostics` and confirm the variant set still round-trips and the codes::ALL coverage test still passes.
Re-run `grep -rni 'xp3|kirikiri|kag|rgss|rpgmaker|reallive|siglus' crates/utsushi-core/src/embed/` and confirm the result is empty.

proposedDagNode.title: Engine-neutralize UTSUSHI-024 embed diagnostics test fixture
proposedDagNode.summary: Replace the engine-evocative `"reallive"` string in the `EmbedError::SnapshotAdapterIdMismatch` test fixture at `crates/utsushi-core/src/embed/diagnostics.rs:224` with an engine-neutral string so the audit-focus grep `'xp3|kirikiri|kag|rgss|rpgmaker|reallive|siglus'` returns empty across the whole embed module.
deliverables: One-line edit replacing `"reallive"` in the diagnostics test fixture with `"other-adapter"` (or equivalent engine-neutral string).
Confirmation grep run in the audit report verifying zero hits across `crates/utsushi-core/src/embed/`.
acceptanceCriteria: The audit-focus grep returns zero hits across `crates/utsushi-core/src/embed/`.
`cargo test -p utsushi-core embed::diagnostics` continues to pass.
auditFocus: Embed module must not reference any engine subproject by name, even in test data.
Audit-focus grep posture stays strict end-to-end.
